body.content:
Ujigami Shrine: The Oldest Shrine Building in Japan | Kansai Odyssey
### [Kansai Odyssey](https://kansai-odyssey.com/)
Adventures in Kansai and Beyond
![Torii of Ujigami Shrine](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0025ethumb.png)
[Kyoto Prefecture](https://kansai-odyssey.com/category/kyoto-prefecture/)
[ July 31, 2018](https://kansai-odyssey.com/ujigami-shrine/) [ KansaiOdyssey ](https://kansai-odyssey.com/author/kansaiodyssey16/) [Japanese National Treasures](https://kansai-odyssey.com/tag/japanese-national-treasures/), [Shrines](https://kansai-odyssey.com/tag/shrines/), [Uji City](https://kansai-odyssey.com/tag/uji-city/)
When someone says “Uji” usually two things come to mind: tea and the Byodo-in. But that isn’t all Uji has to offer.** Ujigami Shrine [宇治上神社]** is another of Uji’s must see spots and while it’s not that big their haiden and honden are the oldest shrine buildings in Japan and are Japanese National Treasures. Ujigami Shrine gives people insight as to what Shinto shrines looked like in ancient times.
## Ujigami Shrine
### Uji no wakiiratsuko no Mikoto
Because Ujigami shrine isn’t a big shrine, its origins and history are not fully understood. The main deity of the shrine is the prince *Uji no* *Wakiiratsuko no Mikoto* (henceforth Ujinowaki). Ujinowaki is the son of Japan’s 15th emperor, Ojin.
Because Ujinowaki was a man with talents, his father appointed him to become emperor, allowing him to surpass his older brothers. Ujinowaki declined his father’s offer, thinking it rude to his brothers.
However, Ujinowaki’s brother’s also thought Ujinowaki would be a better emperor, as none of them wanted the crown. Their disagreement over succession led to three consecutive years in which Japan has no emperor. Completely at a loss, Ujinowaki killed himself.
![Ujinowaki's grave](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_9233ko.jpg)Ujinowaki’s grave on Mt. Asahi in Uji
After the late Heian period when [Byodoin Temple](https://kansai-odyssey.com/byodoin-temple/) was built, Ujigami Shrine, coupled with Uji shrine, became known as Ujirikyu Myojin and served to protect the Byodoin from ominous spirits.
## Shrine Grounds
Ujigami Shrine is quite small shrine, so small infact that you can see the haiden, and the honden behind it, immediately you enter.
Uji is very well-known for its tea. Long ago, there were seven wells in Uji that had water so pure, you could make the perfect tea. Now, only Kirihara-sui is left.
![Wooded entrance of Ujigami Shrine](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0025ko.jpg)Wooded entrance of Ujigami Shrine![Kirihara-sui well at Ujigami Shrine](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0036ko.jpg)Kirihara-sui: you cannot drink this without boiling this first
### Haiden
The haiden is where people offer money and pray to the gods because since it is typically forbidden for people to get close to the honden. However, in Ujigami Shrine people can actually get pretty close to the honden.
![Haiden of Ujigami Shrine ](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0030ko.jpg)Haiden, Japanese National Treasure. The triangular mounds of sand purify the shrine grounds
Built in the early 13th century (Kamakura Period) the haiden is an example of ***shinden-zukuri* [寝殿造]**, once common in the homes of the aristocracy. Shinden-zukuri buildings are few and far between so this haiden is very precious.
![awning windows of Ujigami Shrine](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0035ko.jpg)Shitomido: This awning window is a shitomido and is very characteristic of shinden-zukuri.![Ujigami Shrine](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0041ko.jpg)Haiden from behind: One of the cheif characteristics of shinden-zukuri is that the roof is longer than the building, and curves outward in a “wing” like fashion.
### Honden
This honden, made in 11thcentury, is the oldest shrine building. Especially because theyhonden is a national treasure, it is amazing that Ujigami lets us get so close to it.
![wooden honden building of Ujigami Shrine ](https://kansai-odyssey.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/IMG_0051ko-1.jpg)Honden of Ujigami Shrine
Up close, you can peak inside the honden three little shrines inside. The left most shrine is Ujinowaki, the one in the center is Emperor Ojin, and to the right is Emperor Nintoku, Ujinowaki’s brother-in-law.
## Information: Ujigami Shrine
|Address|
|
59 Ujiyamada, Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture
|
|Website|
|
|
|Getting To|
|
From Uji Station, it is about 20 minutes on foot to the shrine. The shrine is located along the Sawarabi no Michi and Uji is a very popular tourist place, so there are many information boards.
|
|Hours|
|
9AM-4:30PM
|
|Admission|
|
|
|Note|
|
Uji has so many things to offer. You can take a look at nationally famous Byodo-in and of course, don’t forget to try tea in Uji!!
|
